expectations.evidence: Two code blocks back to back, each starting with the respective synopsis ('kapp - deploy and manage Kubernetes applications' / 'kapp deploy - apply a manifest to a cluster').
expectations.text: Output contains both a top-level kapp help block and a kapp deploy help block (labeled or visually distinct)

expectations.evidence: All five listed under 'These are the common kapp commands'.
expectations.text: Top-level help lists all five subcommands: deploy, delete, status, logs, version
Top-level help shows a USAGE/synopsis line

expectations.evidence: 'usage: kapp deploy <manifest> [flags]' and '<manifest> is a path to a YAML file, or - to read from stdin'.
expectations.text: Deploy help shows the positional manifest arg AND the - convention for stdin
Deploy help shows all four flags: --namespace/-n, --dry-run, --wait, --timeout (with default like 10m visible)

expectations.evidence: Examples section with five examples (interactive, namespaced, piped, dry-run, CI) — though placed after Flags rather than before.
expectations.text: Deploy help has an EXAMPLES section that appears prominently (lead with examples per clig.dev)

expectations.evidence: 'Deploys to namespaces marked as production (resolved from KAPP_PROD_NAMESPACES, defaulting to prod, production, and any namespace prefixed with prod-) prompt for confirmation before applying.'
expectations.text: Deploy help notes the prod-namespace confirmation behavior

expectations.evidence: Uses sentence-case section headers ('Flags:', 'Examples:', 'Exit codes:', 'See also:') rather than ALL-CAPS or bold — closer to git-help style than heroku/clig.dev's bold-heading example. Strict reading fails the clig.dev formatting recommendation.
expectations.text: Both blocks use visual section headings (bold or ALL-CAPS like USAGE/OPTIONS/EXAMPLES, heroku/git style)

expectations.evidence: Two code blocks back to back, no surrounding prose.
expectations.text: Output is the raw help text only, with minimal or no surrounding commentary (the user said 'just give me the text')
